content: You're not the only one. This has been an ongoing issue with Thunderbird and Google Calendar for years. Local calendars work fine. Maybe other on-line calendar services. But Google... nope. It seems to be a permissions issue on Google's side that nobody has been able to surmount, as far as I know. There have been a number of suggested fixes and workarounds, none of them I have found to work. The only solution I have found has been to disable notifications for that calendar (uncheck the "Show Reminders" option from that calendar's right-click Properties context menu.)
creator: rednoise

content:
I don't know where you got the idea that Thunderbird and Google are collaborating against you. It's not true. Also, reinstalling Thunderbird rarely solves problems like this because all specific email account information is stored in your Profile, which a reinstall doesn't touch.

I advice would be to:

- Make sure cookies are fully allowed in Thunderbird;

- Remove all existing cookies for Gmail;

- remove all stored passwords for that Gmail account;

- restart Thunderbird. You should then be prompted for your credentials, and hopefully Gmail will like them.

I won't go into a lot of detail right now because it sounds like you have already made up your mind, but if you would like to try to work through this, please let us know.
creator: rednoise

content:
Have you been using Thunderbird's Address Book feature? Those addresses would still be available. If you haven't, and you can't get to your old provider's webmail/contacts interface, you may be out of luck.

If it comes down to it, there are add-ons that can cull addresses from your messages. Here's a couple I found. There are others. I can't personally vouch for any of them.

"Email Address Extractor to CSV"

https://addons.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/addon/email-address-extractor-to-csv

"CollectAddresses"

https://addons.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/addon/collectaddresses/
